meaning: n.随意,任意adj.任意的,随便的,胡乱的adv.胡乱地
examples: Police will conduct random breath tests.
The information is processed in a random order.
The interviews were given to a random sample of students.
He's the judge in a plagiarism case against Random House, publishers of Da Vinci.
Every day, random people came up to her and her boyfriend wanting to chat.
